result.content:
FINAL_ANSWER: -0.222 eV
JUSTIFICATION: The binding energy (Rydberg energy $R_y$) is determined from the 1s exciton peak: $E_{gap} - E_{1s} = 3\text{ eV} - 1\text{ eV} = 2\text{ eV}$. The energy levels for an exciton follow the Rydberg formula $E_n = -R_y / n^2$. For $n = 3$, $E_3 = -2\text{ eV} / 3^2 = -2/9\text{ eV} \approx -0.222\text{ eV}$.
